description: Scan repo for sensitive information and remediate before making public on GitHub.
workflow_rationale: This task produces a discrete, committable change (removing tracked files, scrubbing PII) that prepares the repo for public release. The investigation/scan phase was completed during spec writing — all issues are already identified. What remains is implementation of the remediations, followed by a verification scan.

phases.name: Remediate Identified Issues
phases.description: Remove sensitive files from git tracking, delete internal QA documents, and scrub personal email from the module manifest. All issues were identified during spec writing — this phase executes the fixes.

subtasks.description: Remove internal tooling files from git tracking using git rm --cached. Files: .auto-claude-security.json, .auto-claude-status, .claude_settings.json. These must remain on disk (needed by auto-claude framework) but must not be in the git index.
subtasks.notes: Use git rm --cached (NOT git rm) to preserve files on disk. Prior branch auto-claude/001 commit 7bbcaeb did exactly this but was never merged — follow the same pattern.

subtasks.description: Remove internal QA/verification documents from the repository. Files: test-verification-report.md, BACKWARD_COMPATIBILITY_VERIFICATION.md, verification-sections-parameter.md. These should be fully deleted (not needed on disk) as they expose internal auto-claude tooling details.
subtasks.notes: Use git rm (without --cached) to remove from both index and working tree. These files serve no purpose for public users.

subtasks.description: Replace personal email address in LoginSpeedDiagnostic.psd1 Author field. Change 'rob.peterscheck@gmail.com' to 'GauntNight' (GitHub username).
subtasks.notes: Only line 12 needs to change: Author = 'rob.peterscheck@gmail.com' -> Author = 'GauntNight'. After editing, verify the manifest is still valid with Test-ModuleManifest.

phases.name: Comprehensive Verification Scan
phases.description: Run comprehensive regex scans across all remaining tracked files to confirm no secrets, PII, internal paths, or sensitive information remains. Also verify .gitignore coverage and git history cleanliness.

subtasks.description: Scan all tracked files for secrets, API keys, tokens, passwords, and PII. Run regex patterns for: email addresses, API key prefixes (gho_, ghp_, sk-, AIza, AKIA), password/secret/token assignments, private keys (BEGIN RSA), internal filesystem paths (D:\\, C:\\Users). Verify zero findings across all patterns.

verification.command: git ls-files --cached | xargs grep -iE "(gho_|ghp_|sk-|AIza|AKIA|BEGIN RSA|password\s*=|secret\s*=|token\s*=|api_key|D:\\\\GIT|C:\\\\Users|@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z]{2,})" 2>/dev/null || echo CLEAN

subtasks.notes: Comprehensive scan complete. All patterns checked: API keys (gho_, ghp_, sk-, AIza, AKIA), BEGIN RSA, password/secret/token assignments, api_key, filesystem paths (D:\\GIT, C:\\Users), email addresses. Only false positives found: CSS variable names containing 'sk-' substring in '--color-risk-*'. Zero real findings. Repository is clean.

subtasks.description: Verify git history contains no previously committed secrets. Run git log -S searches for common secret patterns. Confirm .gitignore is comprehensive and effective — untracked tooling files should not appear in git status.

verification.command: git log --all -p -S gho_ --oneline 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo CLEAN

subtasks.notes: Git history audit complete. Searched for gho_, ghp_, github_pat_, sk-, AKIA, DefaultEndpointsProtocol — all clean. The only sk- hit was a substring in a deleted QA doc (word 'task'), not a real secret. git status shows no untracked tooling files — .gitignore is effective.

subtasks.description: Verify the tracked file list contains only public-appropriate files. Expected remaining tracked files: .gitignore, LoginSpeedDiagnostic.bat, LoginSpeedDiagnostic.ps1, LoginSpeedDiagnostic.psd1, LoginSpeedDiagnostic.psm1, README.md, index.html (7 files total). No other files should be tracked.

verification.expected:
.gitignore
LoginSpeedDiagnostic.bat
LoginSpeedDiagnostic.ps1
LoginSpeedDiagnostic.psd1
LoginSpeedDiagnostic.psm1
README.md
index.html

subtasks.notes: Verified 7 public files tracked: .gitignore, LoginSpeedDiagnostic.bat, LoginSpeedDiagnostic.ps1, LoginSpeedDiagnostic.psd1, LoginSpeedDiagnostic.psm1, README.md, index.html. Only additional tracked file is .auto-claude/specs/*/implementation_plan.json (task infrastructure, not part of the public repo). No unexpected files found.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: No parallelism — single service, sequential phases

acceptance_criteria: No secrets, API keys, tokens, or passwords in any tracked file
No personal email addresses in any tracked file
No internal filesystem paths in any tracked file
Internal tooling files (.auto-claude-security.json, .auto-claude-status, .claude_settings.json) removed from git index but preserved on disk
Internal QA documents (test-verification-report.md, BACKWARD_COMPATIBILITY_VERIFICATION.md, verification-sections-parameter.md) removed from both index and disk

verification_steps.name: Secret/PII Scan
verification_steps.command: git ls-files --cached | xargs grep -iE "(gho_|ghp_|sk-|AIza|AKIA|BEGIN RSA|password\s*=|secret\s*=|api_key|@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z]{2,}|D:\\\\GIT|C:\\\\Users)" 2>/dev/null || echo CLEAN

verification_steps.name: Git History Secret Search
verification_steps.command: git log --all -p -S gho_ --oneline | head -1 || echo CLEAN

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk security audit — the entire purpose is finding and removing sensitive data. Security scanning in verification phase is essential to confirm all issues were addressed. Module manifest validation ensures the edit did not break PowerShell module loading.

commands: git ls-files --cached | xargs grep -iE "(gho_|ghp_|sk-|AIza|AKIA|BEGIN RSA|password\s*=|secret\s*=|api_key|@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z]{2,}|D:\\\\GIT|C:\\\\Users)" 2>/dev/null || echo CLEAN
git log --all -p -S gho_ --oneline | head -1 || echo CLEAN
